messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Compatibility Issue: 'imputeMethods' Not Exported from MSnbase Affecting DEP Package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Compatibility Issue: 'imputeMethods' Not Exported from MSnbase Affecting DEP Package
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Support Team,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am currently using R version 4.5.1 with Bioconductor 3.21 on Windows, and
I have installed the latest official release versions of the DEP (v1.30.0)
and MSnbase (v2.34.1) packages into a custom library.

However, when I run DEP functions involving imputation, I receive the
following error:

text
Error: 'imputeMethods' is not an exported object from 'namespace:MSnbase'

This issue appears to stem from MSnbase 2.34.1 removing or reorganizing
functions that DEP relies on. I have confirmed my packages are valid via
BiocManager::valid(), and reinstalling DEP has not resolved the problem.

This breaks the imputation step in DEP workflows, which previously worked
with earlier versions of MSnbase. Unfortunately, due to version
constraints, I cannot downgrade MSnbase without downgrading the entire
Bioconductor and R versions.

Could you please advise if there is a planned fix or an update timeline for
DEP to regain compatibility with the current MSnbase? Alternatively, are
there recommended workarounds for continuing imputation workflows under the
current package versions?

Thank you very much for your support and guidance.

Best regards,
B.Nag
ICGEB,New Delhi

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Changing the maintainer of a Bioconductor package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Changing the maintainer of a Bioconductor package
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for your inquiry.

As you are the current maintainer, we can proceed with this request

If the new maintainer does not currently have a BiocCredentials account we would need to create one.  We would need the email they would want to use for access (or already have access) and their github id.  We will then reach out privately with instructions to active the account.

Once activated,  the new maintainer should test access by updating the DESCRIPTION of the package to provide the maintainer information and a valid version bump.  We use the email in the Description for communication from the organization concerning the package.  We recommend the new maintainer also sign up here on the bioc-devel list and the support site to keep track of any questions or issues that arise with the package.


Cheers,



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Statement
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Statement
messages.body_plain:
Dear Dr Blighe --

I am writing to acknowledge receipt of your statement.  Methods for deprecating
packages are reviewed in the contributor guidelines:

https://contributions.bioconductor.org/package-end-of-life-policy.html#package-end-of-life-policy

The deprecation process will begin ASAP.  Key staff are absent for the
coming week but we
take your statement very seriously and will accommodate your wishes.

Thank you for your participation in the project.

Sincerely,
Vincent Carey


On Fri, Aug 8, 2025 at 7:52?PM Blighe, Kevin (Dr)
<kevin at clinicalbioinformatics.co.uk> wrote:
>
>
> I wish to make this statement:
>
> Bioconductor no longer has my expressed permission to build my packages on their platforms, namely:
>
> EnhancedVolcano
> PCAtools
> scDataviz
> RegParallel
>
>
> Please cease from building and releasing these packages on future Bioconductor releases. Please regard them as deprecated as of today, August 9, 2025.
>
> Failure to abide by this order will result in legal action.
>
> Kind regards,
> Kevin
>
> Kevin Blighe, B.Sc. (Hons.), Ph.D.
> Clinical Bioinformatics Research

-- 
The information in this email is intended only for the p...{{dropped:12}}

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Statement
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Statement
messages.body_plain:
Hi all,

I'd like to remind everyone that the GPL license grants irrevocable rights
to the public to use, modify, propagate, and convey verbatim or modified
versions of the software under the same license, without permission. See
for example, FAQ
https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-faq.html#WhyDoesTheGPLPermitUsersToPublishTheirModifiedVersions

Bioconductor should either deprecate or assign a new maintainer in
consideration of any impacts on all users and developers of Bioconductor
software.

Sincerely,
Levi

?

Levi Waldron

Professor and Chair

Department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics

CUNY Graduate School of Public Health and Health Policy

Institute for Implementation Science in Population Health

55 W 125th St, New York NY 10035

https://waldronlab.io

Join the microbiome Virtual International Forum: https://microbiome-vif.org

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel]  Compatibility Issue: 'imputeMethods' Not Exported from MSnbase Affecting DEP Package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel]  Compatibility Issue: 'imputeMethods' Not Exported from MSnbase Affecting DEP Package
messages.body_plain:
DEP should import imputeMethods from MsCoreUtils rather than from MSnbase. It has been moves some time ago.

Laurent

[You don't often get email from itsbijayaofficial23 at gmail.com. Learn why this is important at https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification ]

Dear Bioconductor Support Team,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am currently using R version 4.5.1 with Bioconductor 3.21 on Windows, and
I have installed the latest official release versions of the DEP (v1.30.0)
and MSnbase (v2.34.1) packages into a custom library.

However, when I run DEP functions involving imputation, I receive the
following error:

text
Error: 'imputeMethods' is not an exported object from 'namespace:MSnbase'

This issue appears to stem from MSnbase 2.34.1 removing or reorganizing
functions that DEP relies on. I have confirmed my packages are valid via
BiocManager::valid(), and reinstalling DEP has not resolved the problem.

This breaks the imputation step in DEP workflows, which previously worked
with earlier versions of MSnbase. Unfortunately, due to version
constraints, I cannot downgrade MSnbase without downgrading the entire
Bioconductor and R versions.

Could you please advise if there is a planned fix or an update timeline for
DEP to regain compatibility with the current MSnbase? Alternatively, are
there recommended workarounds for continuing imputation workflows under the
current package versions?

Thank you very much for your support and guidance.

Best regards,
B.Nag
ICGEB,New Delhi

Laurent

How did I get there?

$ git clone git at github.com:lgatto/RforProteomics.git
$ cd RforProteomics
$ git remote add bioc git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/RforProteomics.git

Any idea?

Laurent

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bump version push error
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bump version push error
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,
I made a mistake in the bump version. I pushed 1.0.4 to my release of
BulkSignalR whereas it should have been 1.0.3 . The website always displays
1.0.2 version. So then I tried to push the correct version but it fails.
How to correct this ?








*remote: Error: Illegal version bump from '1.0.4' to '1.0.3'.remote:
remote: Check http://contributions.bioconductor.org/versionnum.html
<http://contributions.bioconductor.org/versionnum.html>remote: for
details.remote: To git.bioconductor.org:packages/BulkSignalR ! [remote
rejected] RELEASE_3_21 -> RELEASE_3_21 (pre-receive hook declined)error:
failed to push some refs to 'git.bioconductor.org:packages/BulkSignalR'*

Thanks.

-- 
*Jean-Philippe Villemin   *- Bioinformatics, PhD -

*Precision Imaging as a New Key in Cancer Care (PINKcc Lab)*

Institut de Recherche en Canc?rologie de Montpellier - Inserm U1194
208 avenue des Apothicaires
34298 Montpellier cedex 5 FRANCE
*jpvillemin at gmail.com <jpvillemin at gmail.com>*

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bump version push error
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bump version push error
messages.body_plain:
The release builders report twice a week;  they are in process of building/checking the package with the new version and will report and progagate today (see release package build schedule https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/) which will update the website display to 1.0.4.   Given that it is likely to pass we can not rollback a version number to a lower number as R and Bioc install.packages does not allow this.   The best option is to leave the release version as 1.0.4 and incremement normally moving forward (1.0.5, 1.0.6, etc).


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.body_plain:
So it looks like you were able to push the changes into the repo as I can see changes at git.bioconductor.org and a valid version bump to 1.47.1 which is good.

These commits seem to be the commits to make a "tag" on your github for the version.  As we don't have tagging on git.biocnductor.org I believe it is showing these commits as blocked for that reason.

Cheers,


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.body_plain:
Could we please have Pedro as the current maintainer confirm this request?  Then we can proceed.


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Anil Shantilal Thanki <anilthanki at ebi.ac.uk>
Sent: Wednesday, August 13, 2025 5:32 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Cc: Pedro Madrigal <pmadrigal at ebi.ac.uk>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to be Added as Maintainer for ExpressionAtlas

Hi There

I?m currently taking over maintenance of the ExpressionAtlas package.
Could you please add me as a maintainer so I can obtain authentication to push code for the next release?

Details:

Name: Anil Thanki

Email: anilthanki at ebi.ac.uk <mailto:anilthanki at ebi.ac.uk>
This will help ensure a smooth handover process for the upcoming release.

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,

Anil


Anil S. Thanki, PhD
Senior Bioinformatician ? Functional Genomics
European Bioinformatics Institute (EMBL-EBI)
Wellcome Trust Genome Campus
Hinxton, Cambridge, CB10 1SD, UK

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] New database for AnnotationData package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] New database for AnnotationData package
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I have a question: we have developed the new version of the database
for our synaptome.data/synaptome.db packages. This version has a
different API and an extended set of data; however, we would like to
preserve access to the old database because some results would look
different between versions.

Is it possible to keep the old version of the package in the new
release, together with the new one? If not, what would be the
Bioconductor policy about this?

Cheers,
Anatoly

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] New database for AnnotationData package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] New database for AnnotationData package
messages.body_plain:
Hi, Anatoly.

I don?t believe there is a mechanism to maintain two versions of a data package in a single Bioconductor release.

Consider refactoring your database to expose versioned views to the user rather than tables. All end-user data in the database are exposed as ?versioned views? (create view as ?) of the underlying database tables. The database can then store any tables you like and you just need to ensure that v1_?. and v2_? do not ever change the information returned to the user. If you add more tables or information in the future, ensure that the v1_? and v2_? return the same information as before, but you can move on to v3_?.

In this way, the ?data? are versioned by the package version (the information in the backing tables is versioned by the database file), but the schema are versioned inside the database. Of course, you can write your R code, then, to check that the database contains the correct version to ensure that a user doesn?t try to use an old version that doesn?t have the correctly versioned views. If there is R code meant for v2, it will work for all future versions of the database, but R code written for v4 will not work with a database (perhaps a historical copy) that doesn?t have v4 views; this would allow a user to run code using the ?old? database and repeat the code using the ?new? database with new data with the code being unchanged.

There are, of course, other approaches to consider. Happy to discuss further?.

Sean

From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Anatoly Sorokin <lptolik at gmail.com>
Date: Tuesday, August 19, 2025 at 10:46
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] New database for AnnotationData package

Hi,

I have a question: we have developed the new version of the database
for our synaptome.data/synaptome.db packages. This version has a
different API and an extended set of data; however, we would like to
preserve access to the old database because some results would look
different between versions.

Is it possible to keep the old version of the package in the new
release, together with the new one? If not, what would be the
Bioconductor policy about this?

Cheers,
Anatoly

https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/bioc-devel

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: EuroBioC2025 Registration Closes August 28
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Reminder: EuroBioC2025 Registration Closes August 28
messages.body_plain:
Just a gentle reminder that registration for EuroBioC2025 and the Bioconductor Carpentry workshops in Barcelona closes August 28.

You can find details and register here:

https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/<https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/>

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Maria.Doyle <Maria.Doyle at ul.ie>
Sent: Wednesday, July 30, 2025 9:00 am
To: bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] EuroBioC2025: Registration Deadline extended to August 28

CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the University of Limerick. Do not click on links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender's email address and know the content is safe. Use the Report Phishing button to report suspicious emails.



Registration for EuroBioC 2025 and the pre-conference Bioconductor Carpentry workshops has been extended to August 28.

Please note that registration will close earlier if the maximum number of participants is reached

If you haven?t registered yet, now?s a great time to do so.

More info and registration: https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/<https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/>

Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

School of Medicine,
University of Limerick, Limerick, V94 T9PX
Ireland
Email: maria.doyle at ul.ie<mailto:maria.doyle at ul.ie>
Phone (office): +353 61 234 768
Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   EuroBioC 2025 (September 17-19, Barcelona, Spain)<https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/<https://eurobioc2025.bioconductor.org/>>

I work flexible hours across several time zones. I don't expect you to read or respond to my emails outside of your normal working hours.
